perturbation.original_text: "Advertising Revenue Share" has the meaning assigned to it in Section 7.1. "Advertising Services" means the advertising and promotional services (including the Digital Content Service, the Digital Carousel, the Traditional Content Program, Lobby Promotions and Event Sponsorships) as described in Part A of Exhibit A.
perturbation.changed_text: "Advertising Revenue Share" has the meaning assigned to it in Section 7.1. "Marketing Services" means the advertising and promotional services (including the Digital Content Service, the Digital Carousel, the Traditional Content Program, Lobby Promotions and Event Sponsorships) as described in Part A of Exhibit A.
perturbation.explanation: The change of 'Advertising Services' to 'Marketing Services' introduces ambiguity. Throughout the document, obligations and rights are tied to 'Advertising Services'. Now, if a conflict arises regarding the provision of these services, it's unclear if 'Marketing Services' encompasses the same set of responsibilities, potentially invalidating previous obligations tied to a undefined term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: During the Term, except as expressly provided in this Agreement, including Section 3.6 (Policy Trailer; Branded Slots); those provisions of Part A of Exhibit A that permit Network Affiliate to engage in certain Lobby Promotions; Section 3.11 (Grand Openings, Employee Uniforms), collectively, the "Exclusivity Exceptions", Network Affiliate shall subscribe for and NCM shall be the exclusive provider to the theatres of the services specifically set forth in the definition of the "Service." Except as permitted by the Exclusivity Exceptions, during the Term, Network Affiliate shall neither engage nor permit a third party (excluding third party designees of NCM as provided hereunder) to provide, or itself provide, to any of Network Affiliate's theatres any of the services specifically set forth in the definition of Service. Subject only to the Exclusivity Exceptions, NCM shall be Network Affiliate's exclusive representative with respect to the procurement of Inventory (including without limitation all on-screen advertising) for the Advertising Services. NCM shall be responsible, at its own expense, for the coordination and administration of Inventory placement, whether nationally, regionally or locally, including without limitation the acceptance of insertion orders, invoicing advertisers and other content providers, and the acceptance and collection of payments therefrom. Any Inventory which has not been sold as of the date for its scheduled exhibition shall be allocated to make goods, remnant advertising, and other revenue- generating advertising. Nothing in this Agreement shall limit or affect (i) NCM's ability to contract or enter into any relationship with any Person or entity for any product, service, or otherwise, whether or not similar to any products or services provided by NCM under this Agreement, or (ii) Network Affiliate's ability to contract or enter into any relationship with any Person or entity for any product, service, or otherwise, other than the services that will be provided exclusively by NCM as set forth in this Section 6.1 and meetings promoted and scheduled by Network Affiliate theatre personnel as previously referenced in this Section 6.1. All rights with respect to advertising and promotions not explicitly granted hereunder are reserved to Network Affiliate, including without limitation Network Affiliate's ability to offer and sell advertising to any third party on any website on the Internet, its telephone ticketing service or other alternative media sources used for ticketing.
perturbation.justification: The changed text redefines 'Advertising Services' as 'Marketing Services'. The contradicted text explicitly states that NCM is the exclusive representative with respect to the procurement of inventory for 'Advertising Services'. By changing the definition, the contract now creates ambiguity around NCM's exclusivity. If 'Marketing Services' does not encompass all aspects of what was previously 'Advertising Services', then NCM's exclusivity is undermined, creating a direct conflict and in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, Network Affiliate hereby grants to NCM, and NCM hereby accepts, a non-exclusive, non-transferable (except in connection with an assignment of this Agreement in accordance with Section 14.8 hereof), non- sublicenseable, limited license (i) to use the Network Affiliate Marks solely in connection with its delivery of the Service, as approved by Network Affiliate in writing in advance, and (ii) to use the Network Affiliate Marks in Marketing Materials that have been approved by Network Affiliate pursuant to the terms hereof. NCM acknowledges that Network Affiliate is and shall remain the sole owner of the Network Affiliate Marks, including the goodwill of the business symbolized thereby.
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, Network Affiliate hereby grants to NCM, and NCM hereby accepts, a non-exclusive, non-transferable (except in connection with an assignment of this Agreement in accordance with Section 14.8 hereof), non- sublicenseable, limited license (i) to use the Network Affiliate Marks solely in connection with its delivery of the Product, as approved by Network Affiliate in writing in advance, and (ii) to use the Network Affiliate Marks in Marketing Materials that have been approved by Network Affiliate pursuant to the terms hereof. NCM acknowledges that Network Affiliate is and shall remain the sole owner of the Network Affiliate Marks, including the goodwill of the business symbolized thereby.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Service' to 'Product' introduces a contradiction because 'Service' is a defined term (Advertising Services and the Video Display Program), whereas 'Product' is not. This creates uncertainty about the scope of NCM's license to use the Network Affiliate Marks. Now NCM will only have permission to use the marks, when the 'Product' is being delivered. This could lead to potential disputes and makes this licensing agreement vague and unenforceable. Where The Network Affiliate Marks can't be used in their totality, within the full services.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The following terms shall have the following meanings: "Advertising Revenue Share" has the meaning assigned to it in Section 7.1. "Marketing Services" means the advertising and promotional services (including the Digital Content Service, the Digital Carousel, the Traditional Content Program, Lobby Promotions and Event Sponsorships) as described in Part A of Exhibit A. "Base Amount" has the meaning assigned to it in Section 7.2. "Beverage Agreement" means that certain Beverage Agreement, Pepsi dated January 1, 2011.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Service,' which is implicitly defined through 'Marketing Services' in the contradicted text as 'advertising and promotional services' within Exhibit A. The changed text replaces 'Service' with 'Product,' an undefined term. This introduces a contradiction because it creates uncertainty regarding what NCM is licensed to use the Network Affiliate Marks for. It moves the license from defined services to an undefined 'Product,' potentially narrowing or broadening the scope of the license in a way that contradicts the original intent as evidenced by the defined 'Marketing Services'. This misalignment is a direct conflict, rendering the scope of the license ambiguous.

perturbation.original_text: Any delay in the performance of any duties or obligations of either party (except the payment of money owed) will not be considered a breach of this Agreement if such delay is caused by a labor dispute, shortage of materials, fire, earthquake, flood, or any other event beyond the control of such party, provided that such party uses reasonable efforts, under the circumstances, to notify the other party of the circumstances causing the delay and to resume performance as soon as possible.
perturbation.changed_text: Any delay in the performance of any duties or obligations of either party (except the payment of money owed) will not be considered a breach of this Contract if such delay is caused by a labor dispute, shortage of materials, fire, earthquake, flood, or any other event beyond the control of such party, provided that such party uses reasonable efforts, under the circumstances, to notify the other party of the circumstances causing the delay and to resume performance as soon as possible.
perturbation.explanation: Swapping 'Agreement' for 'Contract' in the Force Majeure clause (Section 14.9) while retaining 'Agreement' throughout the rest of the document creates an in-text contradiction regarding the agreement's terminology for itself. The force majeure protections are now only explicitly applicable to a 'Contract' not this 'Agreement', leading to potential disputes about whether the Force Majeure clause even applies to this agreement or a distinct undefined contract, thus undermining the protection provided.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Unless earlier terminated as provided below, the term of this Agreement shall begin on the Effective Date and shall continue for a period of five (5) years from the Effective Date (the "Initial Term") after which this Agreement may be extended on mutual agreement of the parties (a "Renewal Term," and together with the Initial Term, the "Term"). If either party wishes to extend the Initial Term it shall provide notice to the other not later than 180 days, nor sooner than 270 days, before the end of the Initial Term. The parties shall then engage in discussions regarding renewal for a period of 30 days. If no agreement is reached during that 30-day period, then neither party shall have any obligation to extend this Agreement beyond the Initial Term.
perturbation.justification: The modified text replaces 'Agreement' with 'Contract' in the Force Majeure clause. The contradicted text explicitly refers to the 'Agreement'. By renaming 'Agreement' to 'Contract' in one specific instance, the document introduces ambiguity. The contradiction lies in the inconsistent use of terms, potentially creating a legal dispute on whether the Force Majeure clause applies to the entire 'Agreement' as defined elsewhere, or only to a separate 'Contract' and thus, the Initial Term in the contradicted text is not covered by the Force Majeure clause.
